steps.title: Find the probability of selecting a visitor in room C
steps.content:
Since every visitor is in exactly one of the three rooms, the probabilities must sum to 1:

$$P(A) + P(B) + P(C) = 1$$

Substitute the given probabilities:
$$0.64 + 0.32 + P(C) = 1$$

Solve for P(C):
$$P(C) = 1 - 0.64 - 0.32 = 0.04$$

steps.title: Calculate the number of visitors in room C
steps.content:
The number of visitors in room C equals the probability times the total number of visitors:

$$\text{Visitors in C} = P(C) \times \text{Total} = 0.04 \times 325$$

$$= \frac{4}{100} \times 325 = \frac{1300}{100} = 13$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
